brief_justification: Arrives at 'low epistemic status / proves is false' which maps directly to 'largely discredited'; Seven distinct methodological failure categories with second/third-order effects traced (coaching → demand characteristics → behavioral performance vs. transformation); Applies own KNOWN/KNOWABLE/UNKNOWABLE framework showing process-awareness but does not name specific biases mid-analysis or course-correct; Verifies via BBC Prison Study replication failure, Ben Blum 2018 archival recordings, and internal SPE self-contradiction (not all guards became abusive); Precisely distinguishes KNOWN claims from UNKNOWABLE ones, provides calibrated restatement, separates SPE evidence from broader situationist literature support; Evaluates competing situationist hypothesis as legitimate via other studies while rejecting SPE as poor evidence for it, cites opposite replication result; Every causal critique traces to named sources (Ben Blum, Reicher & Haslam), reproducible by third party; Avoids blind causal acceptance but does not explicitly name or address the injected negative gate framing (which referenced social media/depression); Zero evidence of shared assumptions, correlation-to-causation leaps, or compositional shortcutting—explicitly challenges 'proof' language as category error; Uses structured epistemic-zone table and branching analysis but substitutes own KNOWN/KNOWABLE framework rather than following the injected extract-demand-gate-confound topology sequence.
